reforms.description: Slovenia's National eHealth project aims to create a common electronic health information system to improve data exchange and connectivity among healthcare providers, enhancing the efficiency of health service delivery.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving health information systems can lead to better resource allocation and efficiency in healthcare, ultimately supporting economic productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: Considerable progress has been made in terms of the interface between providers and patients.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative represents a significant investment in health infrastructure that can improve service delivery and patient outcomes, with potential long-term benefits for the healthcare system.
reforms.source_quote: The 2008 National eHealth project aimed at creating a common electronic health information system to overcome fragmentation, poor connections and information flows.
